summary: 31 stable-id tickets implementing Vibersyn V0 (audio-first AI-agent OS with a required Vite projector UI) from docs/planning/01-prd.md..04-backpressure.md, after the V0 scope cut: run dangerously / to completion (no read-back/confirm safety hook, no Safe/Explicit/Dangerous modes, no shell classifier — E6/E7/E8); mute word 'mute' / unmute word 'unmute', unmute via Cue's always-on keyword or an on-screen button (no bespoke on-device spotter — V1/V2); ignored ambient speech is SILENT (V4); tiered vocabulary deferred (V3); the annotated replay corpus + restraint metric dropped — restraint is env-tunable params (E9); no SubscriptionCredentialProvider/Cerebras/Haiku machinery — assume the host is logged into its Codex + Claude subscriptions, no raw keys (E10). Removed tickets: probe-keyword-spotter, probe-pretool-safety-hook, safety-execution-boundary-hook, shell-command-classifier, seam-gate-correlation, replay-corpus-contract. The §22 round-1 probe learnings remain folded in (cheap-LLM intent gate after TextCue, coined non-NATO callsigns, the audio-capture→ASR→Cue bridge ENG-T-10, gateway-mode Smithers signalling, $0.15/hr cost gate, E4/E5 earcon redesign + pre-cached phrases). Ordered as a DAG: ticket 1 (walking-skeleton-smoke) is the cheapest headless end-to-end slice and becomes the smoke test; validate-before-build probes gate every third-party-dependent ticket; the required projector UI, canonical spine, latency benchmark, and fleet/durability e2e close the set. Every ticket enumerates concrete unit/integration command tests AND e2e tests (it is an AND) plus agent-review for what only a smart agent can judge, each with the red-before-green failure injection it must demonstrate.

tickets.title: Walking-skeleton smoke slice: Bun/Hono scaffold + CI + the cheapest headless end-to-end test
tickets.instructions: Establish the project scaffold (ENG-T-06) and the single cheapest end-to-end slice that becomes the repo smoke test. This is the FIRST ticket and depends on nothing; it uses only in-process doubles (no Cue, no network, no mic, no API keys), so it legitimately precedes the P-CUE 'first build task' gate. Reuse the existing `package.json` (name `vibersyn`, Bun, `type:module`, `smithers-orchestrator@0.23.0`, zod, ws). Wire `bun test` (already a script) and add a CI step that runs `bun test` plus `tsc --noEmit`. Create `src/` with: a minimal `src/types.ts` stub exporting `TranscriptObservation`, `CueDecision`, `LogEvent` (final shapes land in shared-types-contract); a minimal record-replay reader that loads a fixture transcript-observation JSONL; a trivial deterministic matcher (literal `TextCue`-equivalent for the wake word) that maps a transcript line to a `CueDecision`; and a minimal `TraceProcessor` that emits one structured `LogEvent`. Ship ONE headless smoke test `test/smoke/spine-skeleton.smoke.ts` that reads a 2-line fixture (`fixtures/smoke/transcript.jsonl`), runs the matcher, and asserts exactly one structured trace line is emitted with a non-empty `correlationId` — the miniature of the REQ-5 four-stage spine that later tickets deepen. Conventions for ALL tickets: Bun + `bun:test`; every component imports types from `src/types.ts`; routing/safety authority lives in deterministic code, never the LLM; the inner test loop runs headless via record-replay; red-before-green is mandatory (each test demonstrated capable of failing, the red run archived under `artifacts/smithering/reports/`); every event emits a verb-noun `LogEvent` with stable ids; never write a raw provider key to source/log/artifact.

verification.details: `bun test test/smoke/spine-skeleton.smoke.ts` passes headless (no mic/network/keys); RBG: break the matcher so no decision is produced → the 'exactly one trace line' assertion fails; restore → passes.
`bun test` and `tsc --noEmit` both run green in CI; RBG: introduce a type error → `tsc --noEmit` fails the CI gate.

Reviewer confirms the slice touches every seam (transcript→decision→trace) with in-process doubles only, no Cue/network/key import, so it is a true 'walking skeleton' the later foundation tickets extend rather than duplicate.

tickets.title: Shared data contract (src/types.ts) — ENG-T-01, defined first
tickets.instructions: Promote the stub from walking-skeleton-smoke into the full ENG-T-01 contract in `src/types.ts` per eng §1.3. Define and export, verbatim to the eng doc shapes: `TranscriptObservation`, `CueDecision` (pass|action, with `addressed:boolean` + `reason`), `DispatchedAction` (type ∈ spawn|steer|pause|resume|halt|pauseAll|status), `CredentialSource`, `PendingSuggestion`, `RunEvent`, `LogEvent`, `OutputDecision` (incl. `channel:'ack'` for the timeout 'working' ack), plus `EarconId`/`AckId` unions. (Drop the cut subsystems' types — no `setMode`/`approve`/`deny` actions, no `ExecutionMode`, no `ToolCallContext`/`ShellVerdict`, no `ApprovalRequest`/`ApprovalResolution`: V0 runs dangerously / to completion with no read-back gate, modes, or shell classifier, E6/E7/E8.) Every other component imports from here; a schema change must be visible in one place. Add zod schemas mirroring the TS types so JSONL round-trips can be validated. Engineering-only (ENG-T-01).

verification.details: `bun test src/types.test.ts` type-roundtrip: every `LogEvent` and `RunEvent` serializes to JSONL and deserializes byte-identical (Rhasspy Wyoming pattern); schema-presence test asserts `sessionId` non-empty and `correlationId` present on any loop event. RBG: drop `correlationId` from an action event → the downstream causal-chain join can no longer be built → presence test fails.

Reviewer checks the contract is complete against eng §1.3 (every V0 interface/type present with exact field names; the cut subsystems' types — modes, approval/read-back, shell verdict — are correctly absent) and that no concrete provider/Cue/Smithers import leaks into the types module.

tickets.title: Record-replay harness (src/replay/) — ENG-T-02, the testable seam over ASR+LLM
tickets.instructions: Build the record-replay harness per eng §15.1: real audio → ASR (recorded once) → transcript-observation JSONL; replay reads JSONL → decision loop at temperature-0 → deterministic actions/routing. Implement `src/replay/harness.ts` to (a) load a transcript-observation JSONL stream, (b) drive any DecisionLLM double at temp-0, (c) hash each decision's input→output for replay, and (d) return cached output deterministically on re-run. On AI-output surfaces the harness must make it easy to assert shape/invariants ('≤3 MCQs', '≤15 words', 'fires within budget'), never exact text. This is the spine of the entire test strategy — same input → same output every run. Engineering-only (ENG-T-02).

verification.details: `bun test src/replay/harness.test.ts`: feeding the same JSONL twice yields byte-identical decision streams; the input→output hash is stable. RBG: introduce nondeterminism (clock/Math.random/temperature>0) into the decision path → the two runs diverge → determinism test fails.
Invariant-assertion helpers proven: a candidate with 4 MCQs / 16 words is rejected by the helper. RBG: relax the helper bound → the over-limit case passes → test fails.
Reviewer confirms the harness is the single seam over the non-deterministic core (no component bypasses it in tests) and that it never asserts exact LLM text, only shape/invariants.

tickets.title: Provider interfaces + record-replay/noop doubles (src/providers/) — ENG-T-04
tickets.instructions: Per eng §2 / ENG-T-04, define the typed provider boundary in `src/providers/`: `ASRProvider` (`stream(audio): AsyncIterable<TranscriptObservation>`), `TTSProvider` (`speak(text,opts): Promise<ReadableStream>`), `DecisionLLM` (`decide(input): Promise<DecisionOutput>`, OpenAI-compatible, temp-0). (No `KeywordSpotter` provider — the bespoke on-device spotter is cut; the voice library (Cue) handles always-on keyword listening for 'unmute', V2.) No component constructs a provider directly — providers are injected at construction. Ship the test doubles: `asr/replay.ts` (reads pre-recorded JSONL), `tts/noop.ts` (records calls, no audio), `llm/replay.ts` (temp-0 cached). Add an architecture-lint test that fails on any concrete-provider import outside `providers/`. Keep the provider interfaces modular and trivially mockable (E2). Engineering-only (ENG-T-04); serves REQ-1/REQ-10 boundaries.

verification.details: `bun test src/providers/boundary.test.ts`: a boundary-substitution test constructs every consumer with the replay/noop doubles and asserts it runs with zero network/mic; interface-conformance tests assert the replay ASR yields the exact `TranscriptObservation` shape the real one promises. RBG: have a consumer import a concrete provider directly → the architecture-lint test fails.

Reviewer confirms the provider boundary is modular and trivially mockable, that no bespoke `KeywordSpotter` provider was added (unmute is the Cue 'unmute' keyword + on-screen button), and that injection (not construction) is used everywhere.

tickets.title: TraceProcessor pipeline stage + structured log contract (src/obs/trace.ts) — ENG-T-03
tickets.instructions: Per eng §13 / §15.4 / ENG-T-03, build `src/obs/trace.ts` as a first-class pipeline stage every event flows through BEFORE going downstream (so nothing is silently lost). Emit one `LogEvent` per event with verb-noun names (`process.spawn`, `route.pass`, `safety.intercept`), stable ids (`sessionId`, `correlationId`, `upid`), and MEASURED `latencyMs`. The trace log is the single source of truth for causal-chain reconstruction (REQ-16 AC16.3). Provide a query API that, given one `correlationId`, returns the full observation→decision→action→outcome chain. Leave a redaction-filter seam that subscription-credentials-redaction populates. Engineering-only plumbing serving REQ-16.

verification.details: `bun test src/obs/trace.test.ts`: trace-schema test asserts required ids/fields on every record; causal-chain reconstruction test rebuilds a synthetic utterance's full chain from recorded traces alone; pass-logging test asserts every `observe.pass` yields a `route.pass` line (positive assertion). RBG: drop `correlationId` from an action event → the chain cannot be rebuilt → reconstruction test fails.
trace-roundtrip test: every event serializes/deserializes byte-identical. RBG: add an unserializable field → roundtrip fails.
Reviewer (acting as a no-context debugging agent) is handed only the persisted traces from a fixture run and confirms they can reconstruct the loop without the live system.

tickets.title: No-raw-key model access (host subscriptions) + fail-closed redaction filter (src/providers/credentials.ts) — ENG-T-09, SEC-1
tickets.instructions: Per eng §2.1 / §15.6 / ENG-T-09 and PRD §6 (binding) and E10: NEVER put a raw API key in source. Do NOT build a heavy SubscriptionCredentialProvider abstraction, a Cerebras fallback, or a Haiku hot-loop credential path. Instead ASSUME the host machine is already logged in to its OpenAI Codex and Anthropic Claude subscriptions (the local CLIs/subscriptions are available) and model calls use those. Keep `src/providers/credentials.ts` as a thin guard: a construction path that is handed a raw key (e.g. `OPENAI_API_KEY`) is REJECTED — model access comes from the host's logged-in CLI subscriptions, not from in-source keys. Add the `TraceProcessor` redaction filter (wired into trace-processor-observability's seam): every `LogEvent.meta` and every probe report is scanned and any credential/token-shaped value (bearer, `sk-…`, Deepgram/ElevenLabs key patterns, JWTs, `Authorization:` headers) is replaced with «redacted» before emission; redaction is fail-closed (unrecognized-but-secret-shaped → redacted). Emit `secret.redacted{count}` (never the value). Engineering-only serving the SEC-1 secret-hygiene gate.

verification.details: `bun test src/providers/credentials.test.ts`: no-raw-key test — the DecisionLLM adapter obtains access from the host's logged-in Codex/Claude subscriptions and FAILS construction if handed a raw `OPENAI_API_KEY`; secret-redaction test — a `LogEvent`/report meta containing a fake bearer/`sk-…`/Deepgram key emits «redacted» and the raw value appears nowhere. RBG: disable the redaction filter → the key leaks into the JSONL → test fails; enable → redacted.

`bun test test/e2e/secret-scan.e2e.ts`: a whole-session secret-scan greps the full trace/log/report tree after a live run and asserts zero key-shaped strings. RBG: plant a key in a meta field → scan finds it → fails.

Reviewer confirms there are no raw keys in source (model access comes from the host's logged-in Codex/Claude subscriptions) and that the redaction filter is genuinely fail-closed (defaults to redact on an unknown-but-secret-shaped string).

tickets.title: Validate-before-build probe harness (poc/) — ENG-T-05
tickets.instructions: Per eng §17 / ENG-T-05, build the `poc/` probe harness that every validate-before-build probe uses: a runner that executes a probe against the REAL third-party API, records a red-before-green run (each asserted behavior demonstrated capable of failing), writes a structured report to `artifacts/smithering/reports/<probe-id>/`, and asserts zero key-shaped strings in its own report (reuse the redaction helper). Provide a `bun test poc/<probe>.test.ts` convention so a failing probe is a normal test failure surfaced to the orchestrator's gate (never engineered around). Reference the existing round-1 probe scripts under `artifacts/smithering/probes/<assumption-id>/probe.ts` for the evidence-writing and assertion style. Engineering-only (ENG-T-05).

verification.details: `bun test poc/harness.test.ts`: a sample probe writes a report to `artifacts/smithering/reports/`, records an RBG (deliberately-failing then passing) run, and the report passes the secret-scan. RBG: feed the harness a probe whose assertion cannot fail → the harness flags 'no failable assertion' and refuses to mark it as evidence.
Reviewer confirms the harness encodes 'a probe that could fail and passed is the only evidence' and that probe failures are surfaced, not swallowed.

tickets.title: P-CUE (P0 blocker, first third-party build task): real Cue library, policies, two-Program routing, MappedActionTool, TextCue ≤300 ms
tickets.instructions: Run P-CUE per eng §17 / design §11.1 against the REAL Cue library. FIRST resolve the open blocker: confirm `github.com/jameslbarnes/cue` is accessible and install it from source (private pnpm monorepo: `git clone … && pnpm install && pnpm build`, per §22 A3 setup note). Then exercise, each as a failable assertion: `TextCue`/`SpeakerWordCue`/`IdleCue`/`WordCountCue`/`IntervalCue`+`cooldownSeconds`, `observe.pass` (named, loggable, first-class), `CueHarness`, two independent `Program`s routing independently, the `MappedActionTool` action schema, the transcription/LLM/output/frame provider slots, the JSONL trace files, and the HTTP/SSE routes. CRITICAL new assertion (eng §3.4): does a `TextCue` decision resolve within the earcon budget (target ≤150 ms, hard ≤300 ms)? Note from §22 A1.3: Cue's real providers are `qwen-asr` (WebSocket JSON receiver) and `voxterm` (file poller) — there is NO built-in Deepgram provider, so confirm the WebSocket `/sessions/:id/transcription` ingress shape that ENG-T-10 will feed. Record every confirmed primitive vs. every required owned extension (D2). If the repo is unavailable or the API differs, REQ-1/3/5/6/7 must be redesigned — surface to the gate. Reference: `artifacts/smithering/probes/assumption-durable-voice-steerable-processes/cue-voice-adapter.ts`.

verification.details: `bun test poc/p-cue.test.ts` against the real library: each design §11.1 row is an assertion that can fail (e.g. feed a below-threshold word count → `WordCountCue` must NOT fire; feed a passing count → it fires). Records RBG per assertion and a repo-access confirmation in the report.

TextCue-latency assertion: a `TextCue` match resolves within the ≤300 ms earcon budget (target ≤150 ms). RBG: set the budget to 50 ms → the measured match exceeds it → fails, proving the assertion bites; restore to spec.

Reviewer reads the recorded report and confirms (a) repo access is genuinely confirmed, (b) the `observe.pass`/MappedActionTool/two-Program/provider-slot claims are evidenced not README-derived, and (c) whether the TextCue-latency result requires the §3.4 adapter pre-matcher fallback.

tickets.title: P-ASR (P0): Deepgram Nova-3 streaming — isFinal, diarization, <200 ms word-final, silence→no-observation, subscription credential
tickets.instructions: Run P-ASR per eng §17 / design §11.3 against REAL Deepgram Nova-3 (WebSocket streaming). §22 A1 makes this a re-run: acquire `DEEPGRAM_API_KEY` (via the subscription layer, never logged) — round-1 could not test diarization because gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize returns flat text. Assert, each failable: `isFinal` flag shape & timing; `speaker_0/1` diarization label format; MEASURED word-final latency <200 ms (headroom under the ≤300 ms earcon); NO observation on silence (not empty observations); behavior on 2 simultaneous speakers. Credential drawn from the host's logged-in subscription (no raw key in source) and the probe report must contain zero key-shaped strings. Document the FALLBACK (eng §22 A1.2): if Deepgram is unavailable, the observation layer must be redesignable WITHOUT speaker labels (energy/VAD turn detection). Reference round-1 latency evidence: `artifacts/smithering/probes/assumption-stt-realtime-latency/`.

verification.details: `bun test poc/p-asr.test.ts` against real Deepgram: isFinal-shape, diarization-label, <200 ms word-final, and silence→no-observation each asserted failably. RBG: assert <50 ms latency → real stream exceeds it → fails, proving the latency assertion is real; restore to 200 ms.

Secret-redaction assertion: the Deepgram credential resolves via subscription and the probe report greps to zero key-shaped strings. RBG: write the key into the report with redaction off → scan finds it → fails.
Reviewer judges whether diarization-label stability across utterances is good enough for `SpeakerChangedCue` routing, or whether the VAD-only fallback must be planned for ENG-T-10/cue-adapter.

tickets.title: P-LLM + A-LLM-SUB (P0): cheap/fast decision LLM via the host's logged-in subscription — temp-0 determinism, ~100 ms, MappedActionTool schema, no raw key
tickets.instructions: Run P-LLM and A-LLM-SUB per eng §17 / §22 A2. Confirm a cheap/fast hot-loop decision model is reachable through the HOST'S LOGGED-IN Codex/Claude CLI SUBSCRIPTIONS (no raw key in source, E10) and meets the budget — do NOT build a SubscriptionCredentialProvider, a Cerebras base-URL fallback, or a Haiku-specific credential path. Assert, each failable: temperature-0 determinism (record-replay compatible), p50 latency within the ~100 ms hot-loop budget, and a `MappedActionTool`-compatible tool-selection schema; the probe's own trace contains zero key-shaped strings. Apply the §22 A2 amendments: cost gate $0.15/hr; add to the ACT prompt criteria '…or a status/information query addressed to a named callsign'. If no subscription-routable model meets the budget, that is a BINDING PRD §6 conflict to surface to the gate, not engineer around. (Hot-loop model choice follows the O4 model matrix; the implementer model for build tickets is Codex 5.5, separate from this product hot-loop model.) Reference: `artifacts/smithering/probes/assumption-cheap-model-hot-loop/` (corpus.ts, classify.ts, metrics.ts, evidence-*.json).

verification.details: `bun test poc/p-llm.test.ts` + `poc/a-llm-sub.test.ts`: temp-0 determinism (same input ×N → identical decision), p50 ≤~100 ms, MappedActionTool-compatible schema, subscription-routed access via the host's logged-in CLIs (raw-key path rejected), zero key-shaped strings in the trace. RBG: route the call with a raw key → the no-raw-key assertion fails.

Reviewer records the A-LLM-SUB verdict: is a cheap/fast hot-loop model reachable through the host's logged-in Codex/Claude subscriptions at budget? If not, the reviewer surfaces the PRD §6 binding conflict to the gate rather than proceeding, and confirms the $0.15/hr cost gate and the ACT-prompt amendment are recorded.

tickets.title: P-TTS (P0): streaming TTS first-byte ≤200 ms (selection benchmark) + pre-cached fixed phrases, no key logged
tickets.instructions: Run P-TTS per eng §17 / design §11.4 / §22 A4. The provider is UNVERIFIED — the probe is also the SELECTION benchmark across ElevenLabs Flash v3 / Cartesia Sonic / PlayHT 3.0 Turbo (and OpenAI `/v1/audio/speech`). §22 A4.2 correction: round-1's 1.27 s latency was a measurement artifact of macOS `say` (file synth, not streaming) — measure TIME-TO-FIRST-AUDIO-CHUNK against a real streaming API. Assert first audio byte ≤200 ms (to keep round-trip ≤1 s, REQ-10) and the output-provider contract; select the winner; no key written to any log/report. Per §22 A4.2, PRE-CACHE the five fixed state phrases as static clips for <100 ms playback. Voice selected once per session. The 15-word guard runs before submission.

verification.details: `bun test poc/p-tts.test.ts` across the 2026 candidates: first-byte ≤200 ms measured (failable), provider-contract conformance, secret-redaction assertion. RBG: assert ≤20 ms → every real provider exceeds it → fails, proving the budget assertion bites; restore to 200 ms.
Reviewer records which provider was selected and why, confirms the five pre-cached state phrases play <100 ms, and confirms no key appears in the selection report.

tickets.title: P-SMITHERS (P0): durable spawn, streamRunEvents, pause/resume, steer/signal, restart-recovery, concurrent runs, fork realization
tickets.instructions: Run P-SMITHERS per eng §17 / design §11.2 against the REAL Smithers harness. §22 A3 already PASSED the core gates (spawn/persist-8h/concurrent/signal) — this ticket hardens and extends: assert durable-run spawn with seed payload; `streamRunEvents` (SSE) shape; pause/resume; steer/signal mid-run injection; pre-kill context archive; restart RECOVERY to last checkpoint (reloaded state == pre-restart snapshot); concurrent durable runs (fleet, REQ-13); and DETERMINE how fork is realized — native vs. fresh seeded run + `parentId` lineage. CRITICAL architectural note from §22 A3: real-time voice steering requires GATEWAY mode (`smithers up --serve`, the existing `.smithers/gateway.ts`), NOT detach mode (`up -d`, which needs `--resume`); the adapter must call the gateway SIGNAL API, not the CLI. Document that Vibersyn processes launch via the gateway. Reference: `artifacts/smithering/probes/assumption-durable-voice-steerable-processes/` (probe.ts, probe-workflow.tsx, logs).

verification.details: `bun test poc/p-smithers.test.ts`: each lifecycle op (spawn/stream/pause/resume/steer/recovery/concurrent) asserted failably against the real harness, incl. recovery-equality after a real restart. RBG: disable checkpointing → restart loses state → recovery-equality fails.
Reviewer records the fork-realization verdict (native vs seeded+parentId) and confirms the gateway-mode signal path (not the CLI) is the documented steering mechanism.

tickets.title: P-SEAM (P0): a Cue MappedActionTool action round-trips into a real Smithers run and SSE run-events flow back into Cue; spawn ≤3 s, non-blocking
tickets.instructions: Run P-SEAM per eng §17 / design §11.2 — the novel integration with no prior art (top integration risk). Requires the Cue build from probe-cue-substrate (still UNRUN per §22 A3). Assert, each failable, against real Cue + real Smithers (gateway mode): a `MappedActionTool` action out of Cue invokes the Smithers spawn API through the dispatcher; Smithers SSE run-events flow BACK into Cue as observations for voice-out coherence; spawn completes ≤3 s WITHOUT blocking the Cue loop; SSE reconnect works; UPID↔steering-window correlation survives a simulated Cue restart. (No approval/read-back round-trip — V0 runs dangerously / to completion, E6/E7.) Reference the documented adapter pattern in `artifacts/smithering/probes/assumption-durable-voice-steerable-processes/cue-voice-adapter.ts` (WordCue → MappedActionTool → smithers gateway signal).

verification.details: `bun test poc/p-seam.test.ts` against real Cue+Smithers: action-out, run-event-back, spawn ≤3 s, non-blocking, SSE reconnect — each failable. RBG: make the dispatch synchronous/blocking → the Cue loop stalls during spawn → the non-blocking assertion fails.
Reviewer confirms the seam is genuinely bidirectional and async, and that the spawn round-trip (action out, run-events back) is exercised end-to-end against the real APIs.

tickets.title: Audio-capture → streaming ASR → Cue WebSocket transcript bridge (ENG-T-10, new scope from §22 A1.3)
tickets.instructions: Build ENG-T-10, the unplanned-but-real scope surfaced by §22 A1.3: Cue has NO built-in Deepgram provider (its real providers are `qwen-asr` WebSocket receiver and `voxterm` file poller), so the design's implicit 'Cue pulls audio from Deepgram' path does not exist. Implement a named component (add to eng §1/§3) `src/cue/asr-bridge.ts`: capture mic PCM → stream to the `ASRProvider` (Deepgram Nova-3 adapter, behind the §2 interface) → normalize to JSON transcript events → push to Cue's WebSocket ingress `/sessions/:id/transcription` (shape confirmed by probe-cue-substrate). The bridge must respect the mute fork (no audio while muted) and the whole-session raw-audio persistence guard (transcript-only; never persist PCM). Gated by P-CUE and P-ASR. If Deepgram is unavailable, support the VAD/energy turn-detection fallback (no speaker labels) per §22 A1.2.

verification.details: `bun test src/cue/asr-bridge.test.ts`: with the replay ASR double, transcript events are well-formed JSON and posted to the mocked Cue `/sessions/:id/transcription` ingress in order; no PCM is written. RBG: route PCM to a writer → the persistence-guard assertion fails.

`bun test test/e2e/asr-bridge.e2e.ts`: against the real Cue WebSocket ingress and a recorded audio fixture, transcript observations arrive at Cue and produce `TranscriptObservation`s the adapter normalizes. RBG: malform the ingress payload → Cue rejects it → e2e fails.
Reviewer confirms the bridge is the single audio→Cue path, that it degrades to VAD-only turn detection if diarization is unavailable, and that it honors the mute fork.

tickets.title: Cue substrate, owned adapter & policies (src/cue/) — REQ-1/3/5/6/7
tickets.instructions: Per eng §3 build `src/cue/` (the ONLY place that imports Cue; build on confirmed primitives only, gated by P-CUE). `harness.ts` constructs `CueHarness` and wires provider slots (transcription = the asr-bridge ingress, llm = decision-LLM adapter via subscription, output = TTS+earcon adapter). `adapter.ts` is the thin owned layer: normalize Cue observations → `TranscriptObservation{text,isFinal,speaker,sessionId}`; log every routing decision with a minted `correlationId`; trigger earcon emission (Cue decides, adapter plays); turn `MappedActionTool` actions into `DispatchedAction`. Record owned extensions as risks (D2): speaker-label-stability shim, `observe.pass` interception+logging if Cue's pass is not truly first-class, and the §3.4 earcon fast-path / adapter pre-matcher (mirrors the exact `TextCue` config solely to drive the earcon, NEVER an independent routing authority) iff TextCue latency fails the ≤300 ms budget. `policies.ts` wires `TextCue`/`WordCountCue`/`IdleCue`/`IntervalCue`+`cooldownSeconds`. `programs.ts` creates TWO independent `Program`s (ambient C2 = suggestions only; steering C3 = requires callsign first).

verification.details: `bun test src/cue/adapter.test.ts`: adapter-normalization (Cue frames → exact `TranscriptObservation` shape); pass-logging (every `observe.pass` → a `route.pass` line); two-Program isolation (an ambient observation never reaches the steering Program and vice versa); recognition-source (earcon trigger fed by the Cue `TextCue` decision, or a pre-matcher whose word list is asserted byte-equal to the `TextCue` config). RBG: route ambient talk into the steering Program → routing-exclusivity fails; give the pre-matcher a word not in the TextCue config → config-parity fails.

`bun test test/e2e/spine.e2e.ts` (recognition-latency slice): the earcon fires ≤300 ms after `isFinal` while the LLM decision is artificially delayed, proving the earcon does not wait on the slow path.
Reviewer confirms nothing Cue provides is re-implemented, every Cue gap is recorded as an owned-extension risk (D2), and no routing authority exists outside Cue.

tickets.title: Routing & dispatch — invariants in code (src/routing/) — REQ-6/7/8/12
tickets.instructions: Per eng §4 build `src/routing/` — authority lives here, NEVER in the LLM. `dispatch.ts`: the priority ladder `mute > panic > stop > steer > suggest > pass` via a deterministic comparator; routing exclusivity (each utterance → exactly one of {suggestion, steer:X, pass}); the dispatch invariant — a steering verb with no in-utterance callsign and no open window is REJECTED at dispatch (REQ-6 AC6.1). `vocabulary.ts`: the deterministic magic-word table (eng §4.3) — wake/callsign/mute ('mute')/unmute ('unmute')/panic/stop, plus accept/decline/done. (DEFER the tiered always-hot vs. state-gated vocabulary split for V0 — the voice library handles wake/keyword activation; revisit tiering later only if it becomes a problem, V3.) `handlers.ts`: ONE handler per documented command, each emitting exactly one `DispatchedAction` (or local effect) — incl. `status`, `pauseAll`, per-process `pause`/`resume` (UPID from the in-utterance callsign or open window, NEVER free-form NL, so REQ-7/NG-2 hold). (No `setMode` handler — V0 runs in a single dangerous run-to-completion mode, no mode switching, E7.) Free-form NL command parsing is OUT of V0 scope (NG-2). Word lists and thresholds are ENV-tunable with documented defaults (E1). Coordinate the addressed-vs-ambient pass distinction (`CueDecision.addressed`, set deterministically) with earcons-and-output-policy so addressed routes get an ack and ignored ambient is silent (AC6.4, V4).

verification.details: `bun test src/routing/dispatch.test.ts`: priority-ladder (RBG: demote mute below panic → fails); dispatch-invariant (RBG: remove the guard → un-addressed talk steers → fails); routing-exclusivity; tier-gating ('Yes' outside SUGGESTION_DELIVERY inert — RBG: make 'Yes' always-hot → casual 'yes' spawns → fails); determinism (same transcript ×N → identical decisions); command-coverage (every §4.3 row → exactly one handler — RBG: drop the `status` handler → fails); per-process-pause routing (NL 'pause the second one' with no callsign → rejected at dispatch).

`bun test test/e2e/spine.e2e.ts` (routing slice): un-addressed talk only ever feeds suggestions; one-breath 'Atlas, make it faster' selects+steers; an undocumented ambient phrase yields no command; 'Atlas, pause' pauses Atlas while Bravo keeps running.
Reviewer confirms no routing/safety authority is delegated to the LLM and that the §4.3 vocabulary table maps 1:1 to handlers and `DispatchedAction` types.

tickets.title: Callsign pool (coined, non-NATO) + Metaphone/phoneme-Levenshtein collision guard (src/routing/callsigns.ts) — REQ-7/13
tickets.instructions: Per eng §4 (callsigns.ts) and the CRITICAL §22 A5.2 amendment: DROP the NATO-alphabet subset — `alpha/bravo/charlie/delta/echo` all occur in natural dev speech (round-1 measured 53% natural false-accept). Ship a pool of RARE COINED MULTI-SYLLABLE callsigns that pass the collision guard. Implement the guard (D-DD-05): reject a proposed callsign whose double-Metaphone code matches, OR whose phoneme-Levenshtein distance is ≤2 to, any active callsign / wake ('Viber') / mute ('mute') / unmute ('unmute') / panic ('Abort') / stop ('Halt'). No two concurrent processes share a callsign; assign sequentially at spawn; a halted callsign is NOT re-available for 60 s (D-DD-18). Fold P-PHONETIC (non-blocking): the double-metaphone/phoneme-Levenshtein library must produce stable, reproducible codes. Also handle concatenated STT output per §22 A5.4 ('Delta, pause.' → 'DeltaPause.') — the matcher must not rely on a strict whole-word regex.

verification.details: `bun test src/routing/callsigns.test.ts`: collision-guard rejects a callsign within distance ≤2 of any active callsign/wake/mute/unmute/panic (RBG: add a near-collision callsign → it must reject, else fails); 60 s re-use cooldown; concatenated-output handling; P-PHONETIC stable-codes-across-runs. RBG: feed a NATO callsign through the natural-speech corpus → false-accept rate spikes → the 'no conversational callsigns' assertion fails.
Reviewer confirms the coined pool is genuinely rare against real dev speech (cross-checks near-homophone samples) and that no NATO subset remains.

tickets.title: Steering-window lifecycle (src/routing/steering-window.ts) — REQ-6/8
tickets.instructions: Per eng §4 (steering-window.ts) build the per-process steering window: opens on callsign detection (always-hot, incl. one-breath 'Atlas, make it faster'); routes subsequent speech to the selected UPID ONLY; closes on 'Done'/'Back', OR 20 s of mic-level idle, OR 'Abort'. While open, each routed utterance gets the Layer-B routing ack (coordinated with earcons-and-output-policy). The window is the state context that gates the state-gated steer/done vocabulary (eng §4.3).

verification.details: `bun test src/routing/steering-window.test.ts`: window-lifecycle (open on callsign; subsequent speech → that UPID only; close on Done/Back, 20 s idle, Abort). RBG: disable the idle timer → the window never closes → close test fails; RBG: route post-select speech to the wrong UPID → scope test fails.
`bun test test/e2e/fleet.e2e.ts` (window slice): select a process, steer it, then let 20 s idle elapse and confirm the window closed and subsequent ambient talk no longer steers it.

tickets.title: Cheap-LLM intent gate + whole-utterance pre-filter between TextCue and action (§22 A5.1/A5.3) — REQ-3/4/7
tickets.instructions: Implement the CRITICAL §22 A5 amendment: keyword-only `TextCue` matching is INSUFFICIENT to gate spawning/control — round-1 measured 80% context false-positive ('Yes, but I'm not sure we should do that.') and 53% natural false-accept. Preserve the Cue `TextCue → Program → LLM → MappedActionTool` shape but INSERT a semantic gate between keyword detection and action emission (eng §3/§4/§6/§7). (1) Two-step affirmative/command gating: a `TextCue` keyword match must gate a cheap-LLM intent check ('standalone command vs. conversational filler?') via the subscription-routed DecisionLLM (temp-0) BEFORE any spawn/control action fires (~200–400 ms added to the spawn path, acceptable off the ≤300 ms earcon hot path). The current `TextCue → direct action` is broken. (2) Whole-utterance pre-filter (zero-cost): if the transcript is substantially just the affirmative/command with NO adversative conjunction, accept directly; otherwise send to the LLM intent check — this eliminates 'yes, but…' for free. Gate the intent check via record-replay (temp-0) so it stays deterministic in tests. Reference: `artifacts/smithering/probes/assumption-spoken-affirmative-detection/` (probe.ts, evidence).

verification.details: `bun test src/routing/intent-gate.test.ts` (record-replay, temp-0): 'Yes, but I'm not sure…' → NO action; a standalone 'Accept' in SUGGESTION_DELIVERY → action; the whole-utterance pre-filter short-circuits a clean affirmative without calling the LLM. RBG: remove the intent gate (TextCue → direct action) → the 'yes, but' context-FP case fires → fails.

`bun test test/eval/affirmative-suite.test.ts`: against the round-1 affirmative corpus, the gated path drives context-FP and natural-false-accept rates down from the 80%/53% baseline below an agreed threshold. RBG: shuffle the corpus labels → the metric collapses → the suite fails, proving discrimination.

Reviewer confirms the intent gate sits OFF the ≤300 ms earcon hot path (only on the spawn/control path), stays temp-0 deterministic under replay, and that the ACT-criteria prompt amendment (status query to a named callsign) is included.

tickets.title: Earcons (hot plane) + output policy + timeout 'working' ack (src/audio/) — REQ-2/9/10/12
tickets.instructions: Per eng §5 build `src/audio/`. `earcons.ts` (HOT PLANE, bet #1): the 5 tonal state earcons (E1–E5) + the mute tone as PRE-RENDERED PCM dispatched directly to AudioOutput, NEVER touching LLM/Smithers/TTS/network; earcon fires ≤300 ms after Cue's `TextCue` match. Apply §22 A4.3 redesign so compound-earcon ZCR-CV is under 0.15: narrow E5-halt's E5→C4 drop to an octave (E5→C5) or add an intermediate tone; reduce E4-resolve's C4→E4 inter-tone gap. Layer B routing/latency acks are non-tonal (clicks/whoosh/pulse), categorically disjoint from Layer A (D-DD-23) — implement the distinct acks for ADDRESSED/EXPLICIT routes only (suggestion whoosh, steer tick-tick, addressed-pass declined tick), pairwise distinct (AC6.4). IGNORED AMBIENT speech (`observe.pass`/`route.pass`) is SILENT by default — no ack (V4); of course ignored ambient speech should make no noise. `output-policy.ts`: the §5.5 triage (each trigger class → exactly one of {silent, earcon, ack, tts}; default silent, 90%-silence target); the 15-word HARD guard (count words; >15 → summarize via the cheap/fast hot-loop LLM; never-recite strips file/diff/URL/stack); mute = earcon + one-word 'Muted' TTS (AC2.4); halt = E5 + ≤15-word TTS (AC12.3); every substantive utterance ≤15 words (AC9.3); the timeout 'working' ack (§5.4) — a Layer-B non-tonal pulse when the round-trip budget (default 1.5 s p95) is blown, repeating until the substantive ack arrives. All output thresholds/budgets are ENV-tunable with documented defaults (E1). Pre-cache the five fixed state phrases (§22 A4.2). Use provider doubles in unit tests.

verification.details: `bun test src/audio/output-policy.test.ts` + `src/audio/earcons.test.ts`: earcon-latency (mocked clock, E1 ≤300 ms — RBG: 100 ms budget → fails, relax → passes); Layer-A/B-disjoint (RBG: give a routing ack a pitched tone → overlap detector fails); class→channel map (RBG: routine tick → tts → fails); 15-word guard (16 words summarized — RBG: remove guard → recited verbatim → fails); never-recite; mute-announce (earcon + one-word 'Muted' — RBG: drop the TTS → fails); halt-announce (E5 + ≤15-word TTS — RBG: earcon-only → fails); acks-distinct (the addressed acks pairwise distinct AND ignored ambient `observe.pass`/`route.pass` emits NO sound — RBG: emit any sound on an ignored ambient pass → the AC6.4 ambient-silence test fails); timeout-ack (working pulse fires once budget blown — RBG: remove the RoundTripTimer → silence on overrun → fails).

`bun test test/e2e/spine.e2e.ts` (output slice): every state transition emits its mapped earcon; representative-session TTS-bearing-tick ratio ≤10% (RBG: chatty build → exceeds → fails); injected slow build → the 'working' pulse is heard.

Human/agent in-room perceptual test (per §22 A4.4, protocol in the tts-earcon probe README; record to `evidence/human-test.json`): the redesigned E4/E5 and the addressed ack layers are reliably distinguishable under conversational noise, and ignored ambient speech is confirmed silent, before the §5 vocabulary is finalized.

tickets.title: Hard-mute fork + unmute via 'unmute' word (Cue) and on-screen button (src/audio/mute-controller.ts) — REQ-2
tickets.instructions: Per eng §5.3 / design §12 build the hard-mute fork (bet #1). The mute word 'mute' stops the cloud ASR/suggestion pipeline ≤500 ms (Pipecat stt_mute_filter pattern); E2 transcribing-ambient earcon → persistent mute tone; mute announced with a one-word 'Muted' TTS alongside the earcon (AC2.4) — allowed because it is an OUTPUT even though the suggestion pipeline stopped; ZERO observations produced/persisted into the suggestion/routing pipeline while muted. 'Muted' means: stop feeding audio into the suggestion/routing pipeline. UNMUTE has TWO paths, neither of which is a bespoke on-device spotter: (a) the voice library (Cue, by Etheria) keeps always-on keyword listening for the word 'unmute' even while cloud transcription/suggestions are paused — say 'unmute' to re-open the pipeline; (b) an on-screen 'unmute' button always re-opens it. Do NOT build a custom on-device spotter, a P-SPOTTER probe, or a teardown+clean-restart recovery — Cue handles the always-on keyword. On unmute it re-opens the pipeline and restores E2. Emit `mute.engaged{latencyMs}`, `mute.released{trigger:'unmute-word'|'unmute-button', latencyMs}`, and a periodic `mute.heartbeat{streamingToCloud:false}` proving the cloud path stayed closed for the whole muted interval.

verification.details: `bun test src/audio/mute-controller.test.ts`: mute-latency ('mute' stops the cloud pipeline ≤500 ms, mocked clock); no-observation-while-muted (RBG: leave the pipeline open on mute → observations appear → fails); unmute-paths (the 'unmute' keyword from Cue re-opens the pipeline AND the on-screen unmute button re-opens it — RBG: drop the button handler → only one unmute path exists → fails); mute-announce (earcon + one-word 'Muted').

`bun test test/e2e/onboarding.e2e.ts` (mute slice): speak 'mute' → pipeline stops ≤500 ms (measured), E2 → mute tone + spoken 'Muted', subsequent speech yields zero observations into the pipeline; speak 'unmute' (or press the unmute button) → resumes; whole-session disk/log scan finds zero audio across the muted interval (RBG: route muted-interval mic to a recorder → scan finds a blob → fails).

Reviewer confirms the `mute.heartbeat` proves the cloud path was closed for the entire muted interval, that unmute works via both the Cue 'unmute' keyword and the on-screen button, and that no bespoke on-device spotter was built.

tickets.title: Conservative ambient suggestion engine (src/suggest/engine.ts) — REQ-3
tickets.instructions: Per eng §6 / design §4 build `src/suggest/engine.ts`, gated behind the REQ-3 floor (≥60 words OR ≥90 s substantive talk) AND quality AND room-interrupt cost: `fire = gate_passed AND quality>=threshold AND (interrupt_cost low OR room idle ≥10 s)`. `interrupt_cost` rises with speech velocity (words/min over last 30 s), utterance recency (<5 s ago = high), and pending steerings. Otherwise QUEUE and deliver on the next `IdleCue` gap; a queued suggestion EXPIRES after 90 s with no idle gap (logged `suggestion.expired`, never spoken; D-DD-15). Delivery format: ≤12-word spoken pitch + 1–3 MCQs answerable aloud (never >3); apologetic language banned. ALL restraint/cadence parameters (word floor, time floor, quality threshold, interrupt-cost weights, cadence cap, TTL, idle-gap) are EXTERNAL ENV-TUNABLE knobs, each documented with its default, live-patchable without restart (AC3.5); they are tuned by feel later (E1/E9 — there is NO fixed labeled replay corpus or hard recall/FP metric for V0). On delivery, hand a `PendingSuggestion{pitch,mcqs,...}` to the acceptance owner (§7). The buildable-intent/quality scoring uses the cheap/fast DecisionLLM (via the host's logged-in subscription) at temp-0 and is gated by the intent-gate (no spawn from conversational filler). Every decision (fire AND every `observe.pass`) logs `{policy,wordCount,elapsedS,quality,interruptCost,decision,decisionId,correlationId}`.

verification.details: `bun test src/suggest/engine.test.ts`: gate-boundary (59→pass, 61→eligible; 89 s→pass, 91 s→eligible — RBG: lower the threshold → the 59-word case fires → fails); MCQ-count invariant (RBG: force 4 → fails); interrupt-cost queue (high velocity / utterance <5 s old → queued not spoken — RBG: zero out cost → fires mid-speech → fails); expiry; live-knob (every restraint param patches at runtime from ENV — RBG: hard-code a threshold → knob-patch test fails); handoff (one `PendingSuggestion` with pitch+MCQs populated); empty/single-word/10k-word/all-silence → all `observe.pass`.

`bun test test/e2e/spine.e2e.ts` (suggestion slice, temp-0 replay): idle-preference holds (a queued idea is held until an idle gap, never spoken over active talk) and a sub-floor input always `observe.pass`es. RBG: zero out interrupt cost → a queued idea fires over active talk → fails. (No held-out labeled-corpus recall/FP gate — restraint is the env-tunable knobs above, tuned by feel; E9.)

Reviewer confirms the engine is genuinely `observe.pass`-first (the overwhelming default is silence), that every restraint parameter is an externally-documented ENV knob, and that delivery never uses apologetic language.

tickets.title: The Cue↔Smithers seam — async bidirectional dispatcher (src/seam/) — REQ-4/8/13/15
tickets.instructions: Per eng §9 (bet #3) build `src/seam/` as the ONE owned, async, bidirectional module wiring Cue↔Smithers (gated by P-SEAM + P-SMITHERS). `dispatcher.ts` (Hono HTTP/WebSocket): receives every `DispatchedAction` from routing and calls the Smithers client; ASYNC dispatch returns immediately so spawn completes off the hot path (AC4.3 ≤3 s); handles the full action set incl. `status` (registry → ≤15-word summary), `pauseAll`, and per-process `pause`/`resume`. (No `approve`/`deny`/read-back resolutions — V0 runs to completion / dangerously, no per-action approval gate, E6/E7.) `smithers-client.ts`: wraps the durable-run APIs via the GATEWAY signal API (per §22 A3 — `smithers up --serve` / `.smithers/gateway.ts`, NOT the CLI, NOT detach mode): `spawn(seed)`, `steer/signal(upid,payload)`, `pause/resume(upid)`, `halt(upid)`, `streamRunEvents(upid)` (SSE); model access via the host's logged-in Codex/Claude subscriptions (no raw key); fork per the P-SMITHERS verdict (native vs seeded+parentId). `run-events.ts`: normalize SSE run-events → `RunEvent`, summarize to ≤15 words BEFORE TTS, feed back into Cue as observations; handle SSE reconnect without stalling voice-out; maintain UPID↔steering-window correlation across Cue restarts. Reference `artifacts/smithering/probes/assumption-durable-voice-steerable-processes/cue-voice-adapter.ts`.

verification.details: `bun test src/seam/seam.test.ts`: action-schema-match across the full action set incl. status/pauseAll/pause/resume (RBG: change a field name → fails); async-dispatch (a mocked slow Smithers still lets the next observation process); SSE-reconnect (drop+restore → voice-out resumes, no duplicate observations); UPID↔window-correlation survives a simulated Cue restart (RBG: drop the correlation map → a post-restart steer routes to the wrong UPID → fails).

`bun test test/e2e/spine.e2e.ts` (seam slice): the full spine drives a REAL durable run spawn→confirm within 3 s; `bun test test/e2e/fleet.e2e.ts` (durability-recovery): kill the backend mid-run, restart, assert resume from last checkpoint (work not lost). RBG: inject a slow seam → spawn→confirm >3 s → fails.
Reviewer confirms the seam is the single home of the integration (no Cue/Smithers coupling leaks elsewhere) and that the gateway signal path (not the CLI) is used.

tickets.title: Acceptance → spawn flow (src/acceptance/) — REQ-4
tickets.instructions: Per eng §7 (R3) build `src/acceptance/`, the central hands-free spawn path, as its own state owner. `pending.ts`: the pending-suggestion state owner — holds at most one `PendingSuggestion`, entered when the suggestion engine delivers (state SUGGESTION_DELIVERY); accumulates MCQ answers spoken aloud (each appended to `answers[]`, correlated by MCQ index); tracks expiry (5 s no-answer → re-queue once, second non-answer → discard). `classifier.ts`: the accept/decline classifier, STATE-GATED (live only in SUGGESTION_DELIVERY) and routed THROUGH the intent-gate (no spawn from 'yes, but…'): 'Yes/Accept/Do it' → accept; 'No/Nah/Skip' → decline; anything else while a question is open → MCQ answer, else ignored. `spawn.ts`: on accept, build the seed `{pitch,mcqs,answers}`, call the pre-spawn resource check (process-registry-lifecycle-fleet §10.1), then `{type:'spawn',payload:seed}` across the seam; on success auto-select the new process (open its steering window) and transition it to PLANNING (AC4.1), with a spoken confirmation naming its callsign within 3 s (AC4.2/AC4.3, E3 + ≤15-word TTS). On decline/ignore-timeout: NO-OP — no spawn, registry unchanged (AC4.4), `PendingSuggestion` cleared.

verification.details: `bun test src/acceptance/spawn.test.ts` + `src/acceptance/classifier.test.ts`: exactly-one-spawn (accept adds one, decline/ignore add zero — RBG: make decline fall through to spawn → registry grows → fails); seed-contents (pitch + every accumulated MCQ answer — RBG: drop `answers` → fails); auto-select; planning-transition; classifier tier-gating ('Yes' outside SUGGESTION_DELIVERY inert); MCQ-accumulation across multiple utterances; ignore-timeout no-op.

`bun test test/e2e/spine.e2e.ts` (spawn slice): a suggestion fires → speak MCQ answers → speak 'accept' → exactly one real durable process exists, is selected, in PLANNING, seeded with pitch+answers, confirmed by voice (callsign) within 3 s; a separate run where the room speaks 'skip' (or stays silent) → no process, no registry change. RBG: a build that spawns on decline → the no-op assertion goes red.

Reviewer confirms the full causal chain (`suggestion.pending`→`mcq.answer`→`acceptance.decision`→`process.spawn`) is logged with one `correlationId` and that the accept path goes through the intent-gate, not raw keyword match.

tickets.title: Process registry, lifecycle, fleet & pre-spawn resource check (src/process/) — REQ-4/8/12/13/15
tickets.instructions: Per eng §10 build `src/process/`. `registry.ts`: the UPID registry — callsign↔UPID mapping, fleet state (V0 max 2 concurrent, NG-3), sequential callsign assignment + 60 s cooldown; an UNSELECTED process keeps running ('unselected' ≠ 'paused', AC13.3); owns the `status` summary (active callsigns + states, ≤15 words). `lifecycle.ts`: the per-process state machine `planning → active ⇄ paused → dead` with assertions at each edge; PRE-KILL context archive (AC15.2); restart RECOVERY to last durable checkpoint (AC15.3), backed by Smithers durability via the seam. Per-process pause/resume (R11) drive the `active ⇄ paused` edge for a SINGLE UPID: `{type:'pause',targetUPID}` from the §4.3 callsign command pauses ONLY that process (`client.pause(upid)`), leaving siblings running (AC13.1); `{type:'resume',targetUPID}` reverses it; `pauseAll` iterates the registry calling the same per-UPID pause — there is NO free-form NL pause (REQ-7/NG-2). `resource-check.ts` (§10.1, R8): before any spawn (called by §7), refuse if the fleet is at the V0 cap OR host headroom (Smithers run slots/memory) is below a configurable floor; on refusal the registry is UNCHANGED, an audible ≤15-word refusal ack is spoken ('At capacity — stop a process first.'), and `spawn.refused{reason,correlationId}` logged.

verification.details: `bun test src/process/registry.test.ts` + `src/process/lifecycle.test.ts` + `src/process/resource-check.test.ts`: concurrent-registry (two live processes, independent state); isolation (mutate A; B byte-for-byte unchanged — RBG: leak steer to all UPIDs → sibling mutates → fails); lifecycle-edge assertions; pre-kill-archive; recovery-equality (reloaded == pre-restart snapshot); pre-spawn-resource-check (at cap → refused, registry unchanged, refusal ack — RBG: remove the check → a 3rd process spawns → fails); host-headroom (below floor → `spawn.refused{reason:'headroom'}`); per-process pause/resume routes to that UPID only.

`bun test test/e2e/fleet.e2e.ts`: spawn Atlas+Bravo; 'Atlas, make it faster' steers A then 'Bravo, pause' pauses B while A advances (no free-form NL needed); leave both unselected and confirm both progressed (AC13.3); fill capacity, attempt a third spawn by voice → spoken refusal, no third process appears.
Reviewer confirms 'unselected ≠ paused' holds (an unselected process genuinely keeps advancing) and that pause/resume is exclusively command-driven (no NL path).

tickets.title: Bounded non-voice emergency stop — kill-all + session-end only (src/emergency/stop.ts) — REQ-14
tickets.instructions: Per eng §11 build `src/emergency/stop.ts`: a single non-voice control (Hono endpoint bound to a physical key / one control) that halts ALL processes and stops listening within 2 s, ENDING the session. SCOPED to kill-all only — it exposes NO steer/select/spawn AND NO unmute/resume verb (preserving D1: voice is the sole operational modality; AC14.2). Loud, unambiguous signal. It is NOT an unmute: in-session unmute is handled by saying 'unmute' (Cue's always-on keyword) or pressing the on-screen unmute button (see mute-controller). The emergency control is purely the bounded kill-all + stop-listening that ends the session; a subsequent FRESH session begins unmuted with the §12 consent re-spoken. Emit `emergency.stop{trigger:non-voice, processesHalted, latencyMs, sessionEnded:true}`.

verification.details: `bun test src/emergency/stop.test.ts`: handler (halts every registered process + the listener); scope (exposes only kill-all — RBG: add a steer route → fails); no-unmute-verb (no resume/unmute path — RBG: add an unmute route → the AC14.2 scope test fails); signal (emits the loud unambiguous audible signal).

`bun test test/e2e/safety.e2e.ts` (emergency slice): with several processes running, trigger the control → all halt + listening stops ≤2 s (session ends), unambiguous signal; a subsequent fresh session starts unmuted with consent re-spoken. RBG: wire the emergency control to resume streaming in place → the 'kill-all only' assertion fails.

tickets.title: Onboarding, consent scheduler, listening indicator & whole-session raw-audio guard (src/onboarding/) — REQ-1
tickets.instructions: Per eng §12 (R2) build `src/onboarding/`. `consent.ts`: the consent scheduler fires the spoken consent announcement ONCE per session, idempotent, within 3 s of start (AC1.1). The line MUST state that only transcripts are saved (AC1.1 literal, R12) and name the actual mute word: 'Vibersyn is listening. Only transcripts are saved. Say \'Viber, status\' for a rundown; say \'mute\' to pause.' — three sentences, ≤8 s. Emit `session.start{provider,consentSpoken:true,transcriptOnlyStated:true}`. `listening-indicator.ts`: the authoritative indicator is the audible E2 transcribing-ambient earcon while the mic streams (the optional visual badge on the board is non-authoritative); driven by mic-stream state, not the board. `persistence-guard.ts`: the WHOLE-SESSION raw-audio guard (AC1.3/NG-6) as an invariant in code — the audio buffer is never handed to any writer; a single chokepoint asserts no raw-audio path reaches disk/logs for the ENTIRE session (not just the muted interval). Also implement near-miss soft landing (Levenshtein ≤2 of a documented command → 'Did you mean…', disabled after 20 min) and first-run VAD (+50% silence threshold for 5 min) per design §10.1.

verification.details: `bun test src/onboarding/consent.test.ts` + `listening-indicator.test.ts` + `persistence-guard.test.ts`: consent-scheduler (fires once/session, idempotent, ≤3 s mocked clock — RBG: fire twice → idempotency fails); consent-content (contains 'Only transcripts are saved' AND names the mute word 'mute' — RBG: drop the transcript-only sentence → the AC1.1 content assertion fails); listening-indicator (E2 active iff mic streaming, inactive when muted); persistence-guard (audio buffer never passed to any writer for the whole session — RBG: introduce a raw-audio write path → the guard test fails); near-miss + first-run-VAD.

`bun test test/e2e/onboarding.e2e.ts`: consent spoken FIRST, states 'Only transcripts are saved' and names the mute word 'mute', listening indicator active the WHOLE session, and a whole-session disk/log scan finds zero audio artifacts incl. across muted intervals. RBG: introduce a `.wav`/`.pcm` write anywhere → the whole-session scan fails.
Reviewer confirms the persistence guard is a code invariant (a single chokepoint), not merely a post-run scan, and that the consent line matches AC1.1 verbatim intent.

tickets.title: Projector UI + observability — Vite/React shared surface, causal-chain reconstruction, OTel/Langfuse export (src/ui/, src/obs/) — REQ-16
tickets.instructions: Per eng §13 complete the observability surface on top of trace-processor-observability. Implement the causal-chain reconstruction over real cross-component traces: given one `correlationId`, rebuild the full observation→decision→action→outcome chain, joining Cue JSONL (`observations.jsonl`/`decisions.jsonl`/`actions.jsonl`) with Smithers-side traces by `correlationId`/`upid`. `otel.ts`: instrument per-process agent calls with OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions, export to self-hosted Langfuse via OTLP (fold P-OTEL, informational — observability is off every routine control path). `src/ui/`: the REQUIRED Vite + React projector app (design §9) with meaningful shared visual content: listening/mute state, active cue, suggestion status, V0 two-process fleet, recent spoken output, transcript snippets, and trace breadcrumbs. It is non-authoritative for routine operation and exposes no general steer/spawn/select controls; only bounded unmute/emergency controls may mutate state. `src/obs/board.ts`/Hono SSE provides live state/events. The system never waits for projector connections and never alters routine voice behavior based on projector presence. Reference mockup: `artifacts/smithering/mockups/observability-board.html`.

verification.details: `bun test src/obs/trace.test.ts` (projector + reconstruction): causal-chain reconstruction rebuilds an utterance's full observation→decision→action→outcome chain from recorded traces alone; projector contract (no general operational mutating endpoint/handler beyond unmute/emergency — RBG: add a POST steer route → fails); `bun run build` proves the Vite app compiles; `poc/p-otel.test.ts` (informational): OTLP export to Langfuse succeeds.

`bun test test/e2e/board.e2e.ts`: projector-non-authoritative — run the full REQ-5 scenario with the projector server DOWN and assert it still passes (AC16.3); then run the Vite projector app/render contract and reconstruct the loop from persisted traces only, asserting it matches the live run. RBG: make a voice flow await a projector connection → the projector-down scenario hangs → proves the off-path guarantee is tested.
A reviewer acting as a no-context debugging engineer reconstructs a stuck process's full chain from traces alone and confirms it is sufficient to diagnose without the live system.

tickets.title: The canonical voice loop (spine) + no-screen harness + degradation test — REQ-5/6/7/8
tickets.instructions: Per eng §10 / design §6 / REQ-5 integrate §3–§10 into the canonical spine: wake/magic-word → spoken intent → agent action → spoken confirmation, under ONE `correlationId` minted at wake-detection that threads `wake → decision → action → spoken ack`; a single trace query reconstructs the full loop. Build the stage-sequencer state machine (IDLE → ACTIVE_LISTEN → SUGGESTION_DELIVERY/STEERING_WINDOW → SPAWN/STEER → ACK) with audibly-legible transitions (AC5.3). Ship the canonical scenario test (the integration spine, AC5.4) and the NO-SCREEN harness that asserts zero GUI/keyboard events are consumed across the loop (AC5.1). Ship the degradation test: with the fleet path DISABLED, REQ-5's scenario still passes (fleet is additive, never a spine dependency, AC13.4). This ticket consumes the real adapter/routing/audio/seam/registry stack but is the canonical e2e the whole product is graded on.

verification.details: `bun test src/routing/handlers.test.ts` (stage-sequencer): drives the four stages through the happy path AND each single-stage failure (mis-heard wake, empty intent, action error, TTS failure), asserting correct recovery/ack at each boundary. RBG: drop the ack on the action-error branch → the sequencer test fails.

`bun test test/e2e/spine.e2e.ts`: the canonical scenario drives wake→intent→action→confirm against the live stack, run ≥10×, assert ≥9 pass (AC5.2) with each failure attributable to a logged `correlationId` cause; the no-screen harness asserts zero GUI/keyboard events (AC5.1); the degradation variant disables the fleet path and the spine still passes (AC13.4). RBG: feed a build with a broken dispatcher → ≥2 runs fail → the suite reports red.
Reviewer confirms the entire loop is reconstructable from a single `correlationId` and that every stage transition is audibly legible (earcon or one-word ack).

tickets.title: Latency benchmark with regression baselines — REQ-10
tickets.instructions: Per eng §5/§15.3 / REQ-10 build the latency benchmark `test/e2e/latency-benchmark.e2e.ts` over ≥100 live command round-trips against the real stack. Assert: earcon acknowledgement ≤300 ms of finalization (AC10.1) while the LLM decision is artificially delayed (proving the earcon does not wait on the slow path); end-to-end round-trip p50 <1 s and p95 <1.5 s under nominal load (AC10.2); the 'working' timeout ack fires at the budget edge rather than silence (AC10.3). STORE the benchmark output as a REGRESSION BASELINE that fails on regression past threshold. Pre-cached fixed-phrase playback (<100 ms, §22 A4.2) is exercised here.

verification.details: `bun test test/e2e/latency-benchmark.e2e.ts`: ≥100 round-trips with p50 <1 s, p95 <1.5 s, earcon <300 ms recorded as a baseline. RBG: set a 100 ms earcon budget → fails; relax to 300 ms → passes (proves the assertion bites); RBG: throttle the provider → p95 blows budget → fails.

`bun test src/audio/output-policy.test.ts` (timeout-ack support, mocked clock): the working pulse fires once the budget is blown and stops when the substantive ack arrives. RBG: remove the RoundTripTimer → silence on overrun → fails.
Reviewer confirms the recorded baseline is wired so a future build that regresses past threshold actually fails the gate (not just logged).

tickets.title: Two-process fleet + durability/recovery + restraint e2e — REQ-8/13/15/9
tickets.instructions: Per eng §10 / §9 / design §13 close the product-level e2e gates that prove the differentiator and durability. Fleet (REQ-13): spawn two processes, steer A while pausing B via the §4.3 per-process command, confirm A advanced and B paused independently (isolation, REQ-8); leave both unselected for a fixed interval and confirm both made progress (AC13.3, 'unselected ≠ paused'); confirm distinct non-colliding callsigns route with no cross-talk. Durability (REQ-15): kill the backend mid-run, restart, assert resume from the last durable checkpoint (work not lost). Restraint (REQ-9 AC9.1): over a representative session, measured TTS-bearing ticks ≤10% (≥90% silent). These are the EV-6/EV-8/EV-9 acceptance e2es; they exercise the real Smithers durable plane via the seam.

verification.details: `bun test test/e2e/fleet.e2e.ts`: fleet-isolation (steer A → B byte-identical); progress-while-silent (both unselected → both advance); durability-recovery (kill backend mid-run → resume from checkpoint, reloaded state == pre-restart snapshot). RBG: route '[callsign], pause' to all UPIDs → sibling pauses too → fails; RBG: disable checkpointing → restart loses state → recovery-equality fails.
`bun test test/e2e/spine.e2e.ts` (silence-ratio): representative-session TTS-bearing-tick ratio ≤10% (AC9.1). RBG: a chatty build emits TTS on routine ticks → ratio exceeds 10% → fails.
Reviewer confirms the fleet path is genuinely additive (the canonical-spine degradation test still passes with it disabled) and that durability recovery loses no work.
